title: Unreviewed install script
description: A dependency adds install-time execution that runs in CI or build contexts with credentials.
body: Failure mode: a package update introduces postinstall, native build, binary download, or generated code execution and the review treats it like a passive version bump. Detection signals include lockfile script metadata, package manager output running lifecycle hooks, new binary artifacts, or network fetches during install. Remediation is provenance review, environment narrowing, pinning, and human approval for the script. This dossier complements risk.supply-chain and blocks silent acceptance. Use when a proposed approach skips trust boundaries, authorization decisions, dangerous sinks, secrets, and dependency execution or claims success without domain evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: block the shortcut, name the missing evidence, and switch to a safer methodology or ask for human approval. Validation signals include absence or contradiction in negative security tests, server-side checks, sink payload tests, and secret scans. Risk boundary: treat repeated recurrence as a blocker when it can cause client-only controls, injection, credential leakage, permissive fallback, and unsafe dependency changes.
